nextSession.thesis: PRE-CLOSE (7/16, ~15:00 ET), LIVE into the close of the 7/16 regular session and positioning OVERNIGHT into tomorrow's 7/17 monthly OPEX — the market is OPEN. Structure first: SPY 750.69 stays bull-stacked (20>50>200), +1.06% over the 50-day (~742.8), +8.32% over the 200-day (~693.0), only -0.91% off the 52-week high (~757.6), mom126/252 +8.78%/+22.0% — 5th distinct completed daily bar of the theme (7/09→7/10→7/13→7/14→7/15) plus today's near-complete 7/16 intraday. What the full session CONFIRMS is a digestion, not a break: the tape pulled back from the 7/15 after-hours high (SPY 754.81, -0.37% off high) to 750.69 (-0.91% off high) and QQQ 706.5 is STILL below its 50-day (-1.63%, mom5 -2.32) — the reclaim did NOT happen through the day. The extended leaders remain the weak tape into the close (SPMO mom5 -5.32, VGT -2.99) and semis stay rolling (SOXX rsMom1m -15.62, MRVL -22.82 / MU -14.47 / QCOM -10.59 on the 5-day, 0/12 up-momentum) though SOXX is still +35.73% over its 200-day. The lone structural crack is holding its tentative repair — MSFT 404.5 is still +0.69% above its 50-day (~401.7, mom5 +5.24) but remains bear-stacked and -7.75% below its 200-day. RRG confirms a DEFENSIVE rotation into the close: XLE is the cleanest rotation INTO (accel +2.31, rsMom 102.54, one cross from Leading); XLF is Leading (rsRatio 101.68, board-best); XLP Leading (accel +1.31), XLRE Leading (+1.09), XLU Improving bid; XLI (Weakening, accel -1.55) and XLV (Weakening, accel -1.14) roll out. VIX firmed to 16.52 (from 15.67 at the 7/15 AH) as the leaders gave back — still low/calm but rising. Posture into the close and overnight: HOLD the bull-stack (do NOT add index beta overnight with QQQ below its 50-day into OPEX); ROTATE toward XLE over rolling XLI; TRIM stretched, rolling semis into any close strength; HOLD (don't chase) MSFT's tentative 50-day reclaim into its 7/29 capex guide; carry a small defined-risk hedge overnight through tomorrow's 7/17 OPEX and toward the underpriced 7/29 hike tail.

actions.subject: SPY / QQQ / SPMO / VGT core longs
actions.rationale: The core stays structurally bull-stacked with strong medium-term momentum (SPY mom126/252 +8.78%/+22.0%; QQQ mom63 +10.96, mom252 +27.52; SPMO +13.98% / VGT +14.7% over their 200-days). But a full session of digestion confirms a momentum/semis pullback (SPMO mom5 -5.32, VGT -2.99) with QQQ still -1.63% below its 50-day (mom5 -2.32) and the tape off the 7/15 AH high — hold the structure into the close, but do NOT add index beta overnight into 7/17 OPEX while the reclaim is unwon.
actions.structuralAnchor: SPY 20>50>200; +1.06% over the 50-day (~742.8), +8.32% over the 200-day (~693.0), -0.91% off the 52-week high (~757.6). QQQ 706.5 bull-stacked but -1.63% BELOW its 50-day (~718.2). SPMO +13.98% / VGT +14.7% over their 200-days.
actions.trigger: Hold while SPY holds its rising 50-day (~743); add index beta only on a QQQ RECLAIM of its 50-day (~718.2) with mom5 turning positive — NOT while it sits -1.63% below with a negative 5-day into OPEX.
actions.invalidation: A confirmed daily close below the SPY 50-day (~743), QQQ losing the 20>50>200 stack, or a broad loss of the QQQ/VGT/SPMO bull-stacks.

actions.subject: Add XLE vs trim rolling XLI
actions.rationale: The tool-aligned 2-week RRG keeps energy the cleanest rotation INTO into the close: XLE Improving ↑ with the board-best acceleration (accel +2.31, rsMom 102.54, +11.6% over its 200-day) one cross from Leading, while XLI has rolled to Weakening ↓ (accel -1.55). The sector model's own read this run: 'Rotate toward XLE as XLI weakens.' Defensives (XLP Leading accel +1.31, XLRE +1.09, XLU Improving) bid alongside — a rotation away from extended cyclical/tech leaders.
actions.structuralAnchor: XLE Improving ↑, accel +2.31, rsMom 102.54, rsRatio 99.82 (one cross from Leading), +11.6% over its 200-day; XLI Weakening ↓, accel -1.55, rsRatio 100.01, +8.59% over its 200-day.
actions.trigger: Add XLE on its rsRatio crossing 100 into Leading; fund by trimming XLI on any failure of its rising 50-day.
actions.invalidation: XLE acceleration rolls back negative or oil (USO Mixed, score 40, mom21 -1.58) breaks lower, aborting the energy turn.

actions.subject: Stretched, rolling semis (SOXX) into close strength
actions.rationale: SOXX still has the strongest medium-term structure on the board (+35.73% over its 200-day) but is Lagging on the 2-week accel (rsMom 98.56, accel -0.96) and rolled hard through the session — rsMom1m -15.62, members MRVL mom5 -22.82 / MU -14.47 / QCOM -10.59 / LRCX -10.01, only 2/12 above their 50-day and 0/12 with up-momentum. Trim the extension into any close-of-session relief, keep the AI-hardware core — de-risking a stretched, rolling leader ahead of OPEX, not selling the theme.
actions.structuralAnchor: SOXX 528.43, +35.73% over its 200-day, Lagging (rsMom 98.56 <100, accel -0.96), breadth 8/12 bull-stacked but only 2/12 above the 50-day and 0/12 up-momentum.
actions.trigger: Trim into any close-of-session relief / on a loss of the rising 20-day; re-add only when rsMom crosses back above 100 with breadth (above-50 count) improving.
actions.invalidation: SOXX reclaims a rising RS-momentum (>100) and turns rotation-into — the leadership re-accelerates and the trim was early.

actions.subject: MSFT — tentative 50-day reclaim, hold don't chase into 7/29
actions.rationale: The lone structural crack is holding its tentative repair through the session: MSFT 404.5 is still +0.69% above its 50-day (~401.7, mom5 +5.24, mom21 +1.19) — but it remains bear-stacked and -7.75% below its 200-day (~438.5), -24.91% off its high (~538.7), and mom63 is still negative (-1.42). Per anti-whipsaw, HOLD and let the 7/29 capex guide confirm; do NOT call the bottom on a one-bar 50-day hold.
actions.structuralAnchor: MSFT 404.5, +0.69% over its 50-day (~401.7) — still holding the first reclaim of the theme — but bear-stacked, -7.75% below the 200-day (~438.5) and -24.91% off its 52-week high (~538.7).
actions.trigger: Confirmation = daily closes HOLDING above the 50-day (~401.7) then working toward the 200-day (~438.5); the 7/29 FY26-Q4 print + capex guide is the catalyst.
actions.invalidation: MSFT loses back its 50-day (~402) — the reclaim fails and the structural crack persists into the 7/29 print.

actions.subject: Carry a defined-risk hedge overnight through 7/17 OPEX and the 7/29 tail
actions.rationale: VIX 16.52 is low but FIRMING (from 15.67 at the 7/15 AH) as the leaders gave back all session, and SPY sits only -0.91% off its 52-week high, so carry a small defined-risk put-spread / VIX-call stub overnight through tomorrow's 7/17 OPEX and toward the underpriced 7/29 hawkish-hold tail. Reassess after OPEX rather than paying for a standing short; the exact FedWatch path is STALE/UNVERIFIED and the OPEX gamma walls are not in Tier-A — both must be re-pulled before rolling toward 7/29.
actions.structuralAnchor: VIX 16.52 (low but rising +0.85 off the 7/15 AH read) with SPY -0.91% off the 52-week high — cheap-but-firming vol into tomorrow's 7/17 OPEX and a live two-way 7/29 policy risk create asymmetric near-term event exposure.
actions.trigger: Carry a small defined-risk stub overnight through 7/17 OPEX; reduce it if the tape stabilizes and QQQ reclaims its 50-day (~718.2), roll toward 7/29 only after re-pulling the live FedWatch path and gamma walls.
actions.invalidation: A benign OPEX + QQQ 50-day reclaim narrows near-term risk (let the stub decay), or VIX pushes >19-20 and the stub is already working.

regime.note: STRUCTURE FIRST, 7/16 pre-close into the close off the 7/15 close: a healthy tech-led bull near all-time highs whose ONE near-term crack is only tentatively repairing while a full session of digestion rotates the tape defensive. SPY 750.69 bull-stacked (20>50>200), -0.91% off its 52-week high (~757.6), +8.32% over a rising 200-day, mom126/252 +8.78%/+22.0% — 5th distinct completed daily bar of the theme (7/09→7/10→7/13→7/14→7/15) plus today's near-complete 7/16 intraday. The cool June CPI + Q2 bank beats kept the bull intact, but QQQ 706.5 is STILL below its 50-day (-1.63%, mom5 -2.32) and never reclaimed it through the day, while SPY slipped from the 7/15 AH high (754.81, -0.37% off high) to -0.91% off. VIX 16.52 (low but firming +0.85 off 7/15 AH). Three features: (1) leadership rotating DEFENSIVE — XLE the best accel (+2.31) one cross from Leading, XLF Leading (rsRatio 101.68), XLP/XLRE Leading and XLU Improving on a defensive bid, while XLI (accel -1.55) and XLV (accel -1.14) roll and stretched semis (SOXX +35.73% >200d, rsMom1m -15.62) sell hard; (2) the momentum complex is the weak tape into the close (SPMO mom5 -5.32, VGT -2.99) — extended leaders giving back; (3) the lone STRUCTURAL crack MSFT (404.5, bear-stack, -24.91% off its high) is still holding its tentative 50-day reclaim (+0.69%, mom5 +5.24) but remains below its 200-day — watch the 7/29 capex guide before calling a turn. NO clean haven bid — GLD (score 9.6) / SLV (0) firmly Downtrend, BTC-USD Mixed (44.4) but IBIT still Downtrend (24.2); only agriculture (DBA 53.4) is a firm uptrend. Posture: HOLD the bull-stack; ROTATE XLE over rolling XLI; TRIM stretched, rolling semis into close strength; HOLD (don't chase) MSFT's tentative reclaim; carry a small defined-risk hedge overnight through 7/17 OPEX; stay OUT of havens.

backdrop.macroCycle: HAWKISH-HOLD Fed: funds 3.50-3.75% held four straight meetings. The carried estimate for 7/29 was ~53.5% hold / ~46.5% hike, but that split is STALE AND UNVERIFIED this run — treat 7/29 as a live two-way policy risk and re-pull FedWatch before sizing. June CPI printed cool (7/14, web-verified) and June PPI benign (7/15, tape-inferred/unverified); today's 8:30 June retail sales + weekly claims are the next demand-side read, past the release by the pre-close window but UNVERIFIED. WTI is carried/stale; USO is Mixed (score 40, mom21 -1.58).
globalBackdrop: GLOBAL ROTATION is Asia/global-led but on a local-currency, not weak-dollar, basis: EWS (Singapore) leads at 76.8/100 vs ACWI (rel21 +11.1, Strong uptrend), then EWC (Canada) 62.7, EWT (Taiwan) 62 (rel63 +18.15, rel126 +42.73 — the onshore semis/AI-hardware read), EWU (UK) 61.8, EWA 52.4, EPHE 48.1. Nearly every leader carries an FX headwind / confirms-weakness (EWS fxStrength -1.34, EWT -2.45, EWC -2.5; only EWU near-neutral -0.11), so the equity strength is local-currency, not a weak-dollar tailwind (deep-link: global-rotation-lab.html).

AI-CAPEX CYCLE is the dominant secular driver — MSFT's structural breakdown (bear-stack, -24.91% off its high, only tentatively above its 50-day) vs the AI-hardware/semis structure (VGT/SPMO bull-stacked, SOXX +35.73% over its 200-day even after this week's roll) IS the "show-me" (software) vs "build-it" (hardware/semis) dispersion at the single-name level. The MSFT capex guide 7/29 is the tell (deep-link: ai-capex-strategy-lab.html).

REAL ASSETS show NO clean haven bid: GLD firmly Downtrend (score 9.6, mom21 -7.88), SLV washed out (0, mom21 -20.86); BTC-USD Mixed (44.4, mom21 +7.44) but IBIT still Downtrend (24.2). Agriculture (DBA 53.4, Uptrend) is the only firm real-asset trend; broad commodities (DBC 44.5) and oil (USO 40) are Mixed. Net: risk-on / disinflationary-at-the-margin — leadership is equities, not havens (deep-link: real-assets-lab.html).
backdrop.pricedIn: Cool CPI and orderly bank beats were rewarded earlier in the week, but the QQQ 50-day was NOT won back and a full 7/16 session of digestion left the extended leaders (momentum, semis) giving back while defensives and the beaten-down MSFT bid — a rotation, not a break. What remains uncertain is whether today's retail sales/claims confirm the consumer, whether MSFT's tentative 50-day reclaim holds into its 7/29 capex guide, and whether tomorrow's 7/17 OPEX pin drags the tape. The exact Fed path is not verified this run and the ~46.5% 7/29 hike tail stays underpriced.
backdrop.asymmetry: Downside remains more convex than upside over the next two weeks: SPY is only -0.91% below its 52-week high while VIX is 16.52 and firming, the momentum/semis leaders are rolling, and tomorrow's 7/17 OPEX plus the underpriced 7/29 hike tail sit ahead. That cheap-but-firming vol near the highs supports a small defined-risk hedge carried overnight through OPEX and, after re-verification, into the Fed; it does not justify adding a standing short.
backdrop.whatWouldChangeIt: A QQQ reclaim of its 50-day (~718.2) with positive 5-day (bullish repair), a confirmed SPY close below the 50-day (~742.8, bearish), VIX pushing >19-20, term-structure inversion, a hot retail-sales/claims surprise, MSFT losing back its 50-day (~402, the repair fails) or reclaiming its 200-day (~438.5, full repair), or tomorrow's 7/17 OPEX pin dragging the tape. The 7/29 FOMC is the next major policy regime-changer, but its current probabilities require a live re-pull.

psychology.read: NAMED REGIME: a calm, tech-led bull that RESOLVED its densest binaries of the month benignly — cool June CPI, broad bank beats, a benign (tape-inferred) PPI — but spent the 7/16 session DIGESTING near the highs, with the extended leaders giving back and the crowd rotating defensive into the close. VIX 16.52 (low but firming +0.85 off the 7/15 after-hours read), SPY bull-stacked yet -0.91% off the ATH and slipping from the 7/15 AH high (754.81), and QQQ 706.5 STILL below its 50-day (-1.63%, mom5 -2.32) after failing to reclaim it all day. The crowd's posture is RELIEVED-BUT-ROTATING — the fat left tail (hot CPI / bank miss) is gone, so profits are being taken in the stretched momentum/semis complex (SPMO mom5 -5.32, VGT -2.99, SOXX rsMom1m -15.62, 0/12 up-momentum) and recycled into energy (XLE accel +2.31) and defensives (XLP/XLRE/XLU bidding), while the beaten-down MSFT holds its tentative bid (still +0.69% over its 50-day). NOTE the deterministic composite score of 0 is VIX-only (Fear&Greed was NOT fetched this run), NOT a measured neutral and NOT a data gap — VIX is present, low and firming (16.52). The subtle danger is COMPLACENCY dressed as rotation: the crowd treats cool CPI as an all-clear on the Fed, but the 7/29 hike tail (carried ~46.5%, STALE/UNVERIFIED) is a live two-way risk into tomorrow's 7/17 OPEX and today's demand data. That is the underpriced near-term asymmetry. What would FALSIFY the read: a VIX push back >19-20 or term-structure inversion (regime flip), a confirmed SPY close below the 50-day (~742.8) (bull failure), a hot retail-sales/claims print, the market pricing/delivering the ~46.5% 7/29 hike (the biggest single falsifier), or — bullishly — QQQ reclaiming its 50-day (~718.2) with positive 5-day and MSFT holding above its 50-day and reclaiming ~438 (a broadening that ends the lone crack).
inputs: SPY 750.69 bull-stack (20>50>200), +1.06% over the 50-day (~742.8), +8.32% over the 200-day (~693.0), -0.91% off the 52-week high (~757.6); mom126/252 +8.78% / +22.0% (Tier-A pre-close read, 7/16 18:48Z, LIVE intraday into the close off the 7/15 close)

5th DISTINCT completed daily bar of the theme (7/09→7/10→7/13→7/14→7/15) plus today's near-complete 7/16 intraday — persistence by distinct market-bar dates, not intraday reruns. QQQ 706.5 is STILL below its 50-day (-1.63%, mom5 -2.32) and never reclaimed it through the session; SPY slipped from the 7/15 AH high 754.81 (-0.37% off high) to -0.91% off

June CPI WEB-VERIFIED cool (headline -0.4% MoM / core 0.0% MoM); Q2 big banks WEB-VERIFIED beat — both carried from prior web research; June PPI (7/15) benign but TAPE-INFERRED / UNVERIFIED; today's 8:30 retail sales + claims past the release but UNVERIFIED this run

VIX 16.52 (low but firming +0.85 off 7/15 AH 15.67); Fear&Greed NOT fetched → composite is VIX-only (score 0 = VIX-only, NOT a data gap). Term structure not in Tier-A (re-pull live)

MSFT 404.5 still +0.69% above its 50-day (~401.7, mom5 +5.24) — holding the first reclaim of the theme — but STILL bear-stacked and -7.75% below its 200-day (~438.5); the lone structural crack tentatively repairing into the 7/29 capex guide
Real assets: no clean haven bid — GLD 9.6 / SLV 0 firmly Downtrend; BTC-USD 44.4 Mixed but IBIT 24.2 Downtrend; DBA 53.4 the only firm uptrend (risk-on, disinflationary-at-the-margin)

Forward catalysts: TODAY 7/16 8:30 retail sales + claims (past release, unverified), TOMORROW 7/17 OPEX, 7/29 FOMC + MSFT AMC — the carried ~46.5% 7/29 hike tail is STALE/UNVERIFIED and must be re-pulled
psychology.tiltOnProbabilities: The base case is a bullish slow-grind that spent the session DIGESTING: the leadership index has NOT reclaimed its 50-day and the extended momentum/semis leaders are rolling, so the cleaner edge into the close is HOLDING and PRESSING the confirmed rotation (rotate XLI→XLE, trim stretched semis into close strength) — not adding index beta at the highs overnight while QQQ slips below its 50-day into OPEX, and not chasing MSFT's tentative 50-day hold before the 7/29 guide. The one probability tilt AGAINST the crowd: the ~46.5% 7/29 hike tail (STALE/UNVERIFIED) is underpriced by a relief tape treating cool CPI as an all-clear — carry that as the near-term asymmetry overnight through 7/17 OPEX (watch energy / VIX term structure as the tell), not a standing short. Re-fetch VIX term structure and the live gamma walls before sizing anything off the vol/positioning read.

experimental.title: Rotation-flip detector (Improving/Leading -> Weakening/Peaking) as a state-transition timing layer
experimental.note: Method: flag any sector crossing out of a strong quad (Leading->Peaking / Improving->Weakening) with negative acceleration on a fresh bar, then event-study forward 5/10-day sector return conditioned on whether the next scheduled catalyst confirms or denies the roll. THIS RUN the detector flags an XLI->XLE hand-off: XLI Weakening (quad W, accel -1.55, rsMom6m +1.06, rsMom 99.27) rolling out while XLE Improves (quad I, accel +2.31, rsMom 102.54, rsMom6m +14.44) rotates in, with leader XLK only Lagging (quad A, accel -0.56, rsMom1m -7.16) and no clean Leading GICS sector - a cyclical-to-cyclical (industrials->energy) hand-off, not yet a persistent leadership change (MA stack under XLE still tangled, +11.6% >200d), so it is actionable as a tilt but flagged for persistence per anti-whipsaw. The value is the state-transition TIMING, which no owning tool persists today. Inputs: per-sector rsRatio/rsMom/accel/quad history across distinct bar dates + each sector's next dated catalyst + realized forward return.

experimental.title: Cross-tool haven-abstinence x sector-rotation risk-on gate (non-firing this run - logged as a negative observation)
experimental.note: Method: on each fresh bar, cross the real-assets monetary-haven leg against the sector-RRG rotation vector - declare a 'clean risk-on' state only when (a) BOTH monetary havens are in downtrends AND (b) a defensive sector is rotating out while a cyclical rotates in with no clean Leading defensive; then event-study forward bench drift conditioned on the joint state vs either half alone. THIS RUN the state does NOT fire: the haven leg is satisfied - GLD 9.6 and SLV 0 both Downtrend (mom63 -17.06 / -30.08), IBIT 24.2 Downtrend, no haven bid - but the sector roll is cyclical-to-cyclical (XLI industrials rotating OUT -> XLE energy rotating IN), so leg (b)'s defensive-out condition is unmet; XLV is not in the fresh into/out/leader triplet. Honestly a negative observation, useful only to distinguish a broad defensive-to-cyclical risk-on turn from an intra-cyclical reshuffle. Inputs: real-assets GLD/SLV/IBIT trend+mom63, sector per-name rrgState/rotation/accel with GICS defensive/cyclical tag, bench MA-stack; needs a persisted prior-bar snapshot to compute the state-transition delta across distinct bar dates.
